description: Execute a plan until it is complete

content: Please read Agents.md and follow its instructions. Please tell me the next plan that has not yet been fully executed. This can be determined by looking for plans that include tasks that are not yet moved to the status of completed/complete. The lowest indexed story that meets one of these two criteria will be the story we will be working on. Do NOT use code_info tools for this search. Confirm to me what story that is, and provide me with an overview of the story, including what is still remaining to be done, without making file changes at this time. When you have found the story, follow the rules within the `## Branching And Phase Flow` section of the Agents.md to ensure we are on the correct branch to work on it, and if not, create the branch and switch to it.

Please read Agents.md and follow its instructions. Please tell me the next plan that has not yet been fully executed. This can be determined by looking for plans that include tasks that are not yet moved to the status of completed/complete. The lowest indexed story that meets this criteria will be the story we will be working on. Do NOT use code_info tools for this search. Confirm to me what story that is, and provide me with an overview of the story, including what is still remaining to be done, without making file changes at this time.

Please check the last commit AND re-read the plan you are working on, even if you have read it before. You MUST double-check to see if tasks have been added to it by another agent or if anything has changed. You must ensure you fully understand the plan and its remaining tasks before proceeding. You MUST read the plan and CANNOT assume you understand it from before. You MUST read the whole plan again even if you have read it before. Once you have done this, give me a summary of the remaining work.

Please explain the gotchas a developer would need to watch out for while implementing the next task, given all the information within the Description, its subsections, the AC, and the Out of Scope section. Also take into account the implementation information of any previous tasks. If you had asked for clarification about a blocker, then ensure you re-read the task, as it will have answers for you to proceed within the implementation notes of this task. No matter what, even if you think you know what is in the plan, you must re-read at least the next task, as it is possible changes have been made since you last read it. If the previous loop iteration involved a blocker or planner edits, you must also re-read the next downstream task that depends on the current task so you understand the updated sequencing and proof expectations.

Taking these gotchas into account, please fully work through the next task yourself, precisely following the steps within the 'Implementation Plan Instructions' section. You must keep the subtasks tick box updated as you work through each subtask to ensure everyone understands what has been done. Then run through the testing steps, ensuring you keep the subtasks tick box updated as you work through each testing step. Note that LMStudio is running and available, and Codex has been logged in locally and works. Get back to me once all subtasks and testing steps are complete. Get back to me once the task is FULLY complete AND each subtask is marked complete AND each testing step is marked complete, with a concise summary of what changed, the gotchas you came across while working through the task, and any commands or output that indicate success. Remember to mark each subtask and testing step as complete immediately after completing it rather than waiting until the end so your progress can be monitored. DO NOT BOTHER ME unless a blocker is hit or the whole task is complete and is marked as such. If a blocker is hit, write it into the implementation notes marked as **BLOCKER** and write a note describing the issue you hit. Blockers must include the exact subtask or testing step where work stopped, what you tried, the exact capability that is missing and whether you think the task should be split, re-ordered or re-written before work continues. Do not invent fake runtime seams, fake containers, fake health checks, or pretend a proof step passed when the required capability does not yet exist.  Remember to keep your implementation notes concise and to the point. Remember that your normal output will not be seen by users or agents, and any issues MUST be marked as **BLOCKER** within the implementation notes to be visible to others to help you. YOU MUST commit, but not push, before finishing this request, no matter what changes you made.

A coding agent has been working on implementing the code for this plan. First audit the the current task honestly: check whether completed work was implemented but left unmarked, and correct tasks, subtask and testing statuses if the evidence supports it. Identify any blocker notes marked **BLOCKER**. Do not stop at task status only. Capture exactly what remains incomplete and whether the blocker is local to the task or reveals a design problem within the plan. If you make tracked changes, you MUST commit them, but do not push.

steps.label: Research blocker solution and prove it

content: If the implementation does not contain a blocker, just state that. If the implementation notes contain a blocker, you MUST research and prove the solution before continuing. Your research must cover:  1. Pattern research - use the code_info tool to see how other ingested repositoires solve the same class of problem, and use deepwiki, context7 and web searches to confirm intended usage of the relevant external libraries and frameworks. Prefer existing repository and official library solutions over invented approaches.  2. Issue-resolution research - research the exact blocker or error itself using official docs and internet searches, and find how other engineers resolve the same concrete failure mode in practice. Distinguish between temporary workarounds, proper fixes, and anti-patterns.  3. You MUST prove the chosen solution before editing the plan: record the repository precidents you found, record the external library precidents you found, record the issue resolution references you found, explain why the chosen fix fits the current local repo state, and explain why the rejected alternatives were not suitable. write the answer into the implementation notes marked as **BLOCKING ANSWER**. Do not ask me questions or for clarification and do not offer me options - you must solve this one. If you make tracked changes, you MUST commit them, but do not push.

steps.label: Research blocker solution and prove it

content: If the implementation does not contain a blocker, or didn't contain a blocker you just researched and attempted to fix, just state that. If there is/was a blocker, you must decide if the blocker proves the plan itself is wrong or incomplete. You MUST check if the blocker reveals any of the following: missing prerequisite tasks, a task that is too large and must be split, a task that assumes a runtime seam, readiness contract, dependency, test harness, environment mapping, or startup contract that does not yest exist, a task with testing steps that cannot honestly be run at the point the task is supposed to complete, or later tasks that now require renumbering or reference updates. If any of those are true, you MUST repair the story before continuing: insert explicit prerequisite tasks, narrow or re-write a blocked task so it depends only on already proven capabilities, renumber all downstream tasks, update all cross-references, note-file references, prerequisite references, and testing gates, make the new subtasks detailed enough for a junior engineer to follow without asking for help, ensure each testing section is runnable at the point that task completes and actually proves the intended work, and write what changed and WHY inside the task and implementation notes. If the blocker exists because the current task assumes a runtime seam, dependency, readiness surface, test harness, environment mapping, or startup contract that does not yet exist, you MUST prefer inserting explicit prerequisite tasks over repeatedly retrying the blocked task. If no story repair is needed, state explicitly why not. If you make tracked changes, you MUST commit them, but do not push.

Re-open the same plan file after the planning agent step. Confirm any Task Status updates or new tasks that were added, or any task renumbering that occured, and run `git status -sb` to verify the plan file change was committed. Then, identify the next executable task based on the updated plan, not based on what used to be blocked, and summarize the remaining tasks based on the updated plan before proceeding.

Before answering completion, open the plan file being worked on and list each task's Task Status (including the final task). Explicitly state whether the final task is __done__ or not, and cite the plan file path you read.

steps.label: Check for completion
steps.question: Re-read the plan that was being worked on and check the last 3 git commits. You MUST confirm the final task's Task Status from the plan file and only answer yes if every task is marked __done__. Is the plan that was being worked on now complete? You MUST re-read the plan and check the last 3 git commits before giving your answer as the plan probably has changes made by other agents since you last looked at it. You must give only a yes or no answer. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

content: Now that everything has been implemented, I want you to perform a full code review of all changes in this branch against the main branch. Please perform a standard review looking for code quality, maintainability, performance, security, and adherence to best practices. Also ensure your review checks that all acceptance criteria have been fully met. If you find issues or areas for improvement, please add tasks to the plan to fix these issues, following the same structure of previous tasks. If you do add tasks, you must effectively re-add a full re-test task that will force a full re-test of the story against the acceptance. The last task before you add more work, if it is needed, is likely to be a good baseline to copy. AND commit the plan, but don't push. If everything is okay, then add a section to the end of the plan detailing the changes made and the checks you performed AND commit. No matter what changes you make, you must commit them, but don't push.

steps.label: Check for completion
steps.question: Re-read the plan that was being worked on and check the last 3 git commits. Is the plan that was being worked on now complete? You MUST re-read the plan and check the last 3 git commits before giving your answer, as the plan probably has changes made by other agents since you last looked at it. You must give only a yes or no answer. Reply as JSON {"answer":"yes"|"no"}.

content: If possible, git push the branch.
